evals.name: generic-pi5-readiness
evals.prompt: Create a comprehensive pre-installation system checker script for my Raspberry Pi 5 running Debian Bookworm. I want to check everything before installing any server application.
evals.expected_output: A bash script with all 15+ sections, Pi 5 specific checks, auto-tune section, uses inc() helper, no ((VAR++)), no set -e, passes bash -n
assertions: Script passes bash -n with no errors
No raw ((VAR++)) patterns outside of comments
Section 13 contains Pi-specific checks (vcgencmd, config.txt, cmdline.txt)
Every warn/fail message contains ' — ' with a fix command

evals.name: docker-compose-stack
evals.prompt: I'm about to install a Docker Compose application stack on my Pi 4 (Bookworm). It needs ports 80, 443, 3000, 5432, 6379. Create a pre-installation checker.
evals.expected_output: Script checking Docker prerequisites, those specific ports, cgroups v2, overlay2 storage driver, sufficient disk space
assertions: Includes Pi 4 compatible checks (no Pi 5 only features like PCIe Gen 3)

evals.name: k3s-kubernetes
evals.prompt: Check my Pi 5 cluster node before installing k3s. It's running Bookworm, I'm the user 'pi' with sudo access.
evals.expected_output: Script with k3s-specific checks: kubectl, helm, required ports 6443/10250/10257/10259, conflict service detection (microk8s, rke2), cgroup params
assertions: Checks for conflicting services: microk8s, rke2-server, rke2-agent

evals.name: home-assistant
evals.prompt: I want to install Home Assistant Supervised on my Pi 4 running Bookworm. What do I need to check first?
evals.expected_output: Script checking HA Supervised prerequisites: systemd-resolved, AppArmor, Docker, network manager, specific OS requirements
